description: Material manufactured in thin sheets from the pulp of wood or other fibrous substances.
preparation: Remove any plastic wrapping or non-paper liners
commonMistakes: Recycling paper towels, tissues, or napkins (they are too short-fibered)
Recycling shredded paper loosely (it clogs machinery)
facts: Recycling one ton of paper saves 17 trees and 7,000 gallons of water.
Paper can typically be recycled 5 to 7 times before the fibers become too short.
Producing recycled paper requires about 60% of the energy used to make paper from virgin wood pulp.
